summary: This notebook provides a reproducible baseline for fine-tuning the Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct model using LoRA on a Reddit comment classification task. It demonstrates how to adapt a causal language model for binary classification by framing the problem as a text completion task with a structured system prompt and few-shot examples. The author highlights memory-efficient training techniques, including GPTQ-Int4 quantization, 8-bit optimizers, gradient checkpointing, and mixed precision. Readers should learn how to structure instruction-tuning data for LLMs, apply PEFT/LoRA correctly, and configure the Hugging Face Trainer for stable, resource-conscious fine-tuning.
competition_flow: Loads the Jigsaw train CSV, formats comments into a few-shot completion prompt, fine-tunes a quantized Qwen2.5-7B model with LoRA via KFold cross-validation, and saves the trained adapter weights.
data_processing: Formats raw text into a structured chat template with a system prompt and few-shot examples
Tokenizes using apply_chat_template with left padding
Appends the ground truth label ("Yes"/"No") for completion-based training
Uses DataCollatorForCompletionOnlyLM to mask prompt tokens during loss calculation
insights: Framing classification as a text completion task with explicit few-shot examples improves instruction-following for causal LLMs.
Using GPTQ-Int4 quantization combined with 8-bit optimizers and gradient checkpointing enables fine-tuning large models on consumer GPUs.
LoRA should target both attention and MLP projection layers for effective adaptation.
